title: Details of Board of Peace Gaza Disarmament Plan Revealed
detail: Al Jazeera reported on March 26, 2026 that details of the 'Board of Peace' plan for Gaza disarmament have been revealed, describing an eight-month, multiphase implementation process. The Board of Peace held its inaugural Washington meeting on February 18, 2026 with 27 signatory countries and approximately 50 diplomatic envoys. The plan calls for Hamas to decommission all offensive heavy weapons, including rocket arsenals, anti-tank weapons, and tunnel infrastructure, over eight months. In exchange, a phased Israeli military withdrawal and international reconstruction fund activation are envisioned. Hamas has signaled conditional acceptance to decommission offensive weapons only if simultaneous Israeli withdrawal and Palestinian statehood process begin — conditions Israel has rejected.

title: UK Parliament: 73% of October 7 Victims Were Civilians
detail: A comprehensive UK Parliamentary investigation documented that 73% of those murdered on October 7, 2023 were civilians — including women, children, elderly, and foreign nationals. The parliamentary report drew on verified Israeli government data, forensic evidence, and survivor testimony. The investigation also addressed Hamas's use of sexual violence, noting multiple witness accounts and forensic evidence supporting the UN Special Representative's findings of 'reasonable grounds' to believe widespread sexual violence occurred. The UK report called October 7 'one of the worst terrorist attacks in the 21st century' and called for continued accountability for Hamas perpetrators.
